description: BIS Standard for Skin Creams and Lotions. Includes analytical characteristics like pH limits, and maximum thresholds for heavy metals.

rules.name: Mercury Ban
rules.description: Mercury is strictly banned for use as a skin lightening or stabilizing agent in cosmetic creams/lotions under BIS regulations.

rules.name: Lead Limit
rules.description: Lead (Pb) impurity must not exceed 20 ppm in skin creams as per IS 6608.

rules.name: Arsenic Limit
rules.description: Arsenic (As) must not exceed 2 ppm in cosmetic skin creams/lotions.

rules.name: Heavy Metals Cumulative Limit
rules.description: Cumulative sum of Lead + Arsenic must not exceed 22 ppm in skin applications.

rules.name: Minimum pH Level
rules.description: pH of the skin cream/lotion must not be lower than 4.0 to prevent skin barrier damage and irritation.

rules.name: Maximum pH Level
rules.description: pH of the skin cream/lotion must not exceed 9.0 to maintain the acid mantle of the skin.

rules.name: Preservatives Limit (Parabens)
rules.description: Paraben-based preservatives should not exceed 0.4% in leave-on cosmetic products.

rules.name: Total Fragrance Limit
rules.description: Total fragrant compounds should generally not exceed 1.0% in leave-on facial and body lotions.

rules.name: Stearic Acid Limit
rules.description: Stearic acid acts as an emulsifier and thickener, but exceeding 20% can cause severe skin irritation, physical hardening, and formulation failure.

rules.name: Glycerin Limit
rules.description: Glycerin concentrations above 15% can cause excessive stickiness and paradoxically draw moisture out of the skin in dry environments.

rules.name: Sodium Hydroxide Limit
rules.description: Sodium hydroxide is permitted in trace amounts for pH adjusting, but must not exceed 1% to prevent severe chemical burns.

rules.name: Mineral Oil Limit
rules.description: Excess mineral oil (above 40%) can lead to comedogenic effects and heavy greasiness in standard lotion formulations.

rules.name: Cetyl Alcohol Limit
rules.description: Cetyl alcohol should not exceed 8% to maintain stability without causing excessive waxy buildup on the skin.
